clinicalHub.title: Clinical Support Deferred

scenarios.title: In-person clinical practice deferred

modules.source_status_flag: Generated from approved Module 15 PDF source map.
learning_objectives: Identify word elements used in medical terms
Identify medical terminology and abbreviations commonly used in medical facilities
Define observation and list the senses used to observe a patient/resident.
List types of charting documents and the use for each
Explain how to accurately complete ADL assessment for MDS
Discuss procedures to use when recording on a patient's/resident's chart

Source excerpt support: Define observation and list the senses used to observe a patient/resident. A. Observation � use of senses to collect information about a patient/resident 1. Senses used for evaluation a. Sight b. Touch c. Hearing d. Smell 2. Observations should be made with regard to a patient's/resident's a. Skin color and temperature b. Mood and mental status c. Behavior, movement d. Unusual odors e. Respiration f. Ability to respond g. Appetite h. Performance of ADL i. Elimination j. Pain or discomfort Learn

cards.learning_goal: Discuss procedures to use when recording on a patient's/resident's chart
cards.learner_facing_content: Source-traced focus: Discuss procedures to use when recording on a patient's/resident's chart. Review the approved source outline, use de-identified examples only, and apply report/observe/defer boundaries. Source excerpt support: Discuss procedures used when recording on a patient's/resident's chart. A. Charting procedures 1. Make sure you have the correct chart or ADL sheet 2. Write legibly and neatly a. Write notes on paper first b. Check for accuracy and spelling 3. Place events in proper sequence 4. Chart according to facility/policy standards 5. Be concise and use appropriate terms and abbreviations 6. Always use ballpoint pen a. Black ink b. No felt tip, fountain pens, pencils or gel pens c. Use color only if appro
